question.content: For the four labeled Newman projections A-D around the C2-C3 bond, determine the most stable conformation and the least stable conformation. Output exactly one token in the format lowest>highest using labels A-D, plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equality sign, no chained equalities, and no extra text. Answer format: one final result only. If the result naturally simplifies to a single value, give that value; otherwise give a single algebraic expression using the symbols defined in the problem. Do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Read the four labeled Newman candidates A-D and compare CH3-CH3 dihedral relationships around C2-C3.
step_1.key_point: Large-group eclipsing raises energy; anti placement lowers energy.

step_3.content: Candidate A is anti staggered, so CH3 groups are opposite and steric repulsion is minimized.
step_3.key_point: Anti (180 deg) is the lowest-energy conformation for butane.

step_5.content: Candidate D is fully eclipsed with CH3 directly eclipsing CH3, maximizing torsional and steric strain.
step_5.key_point: Fully eclipsed CH3-CH3 is the highest-energy conformation.

step_7.content: The requested output is the pair lowest>highest using labels only.
step_7.key_point: Combine the two identified labels as one expression token.
